course.title: Responsive Web Design: Build for Everyone
course.description: A cumulative, evidence-driven path from computer and browser foundations to independently designed, accessible, responsive web experiences.
outcomes: Build semantic web documents that remain understandable across devices and assistive technology.
Design resilient responsive systems using modern CSS, content-driven constraints, and accessible interaction patterns.
Investigate defects with browser evidence and verify finished work through automated and manual tests.

competencies.statement: Explain how hardware, operating systems, applications, and data cooperate to run a web page.
misconceptions: A browser performs every hardware and operating-system responsibility by itself.
masteryEvidence: A systems trace correctly connects input, processing, storage, browser work, graphics work, and output.

competencies.statement: Create, organize, identify, and reference web project files using reliable paths and extensions.
misconceptions: A displayed filename always proves the actual extension, location, and file type.
masteryEvidence: A project keeps loading its resources after the complete project folder moves to a new location.

competencies.statement: Distinguish the internet, web, browser, server, URL, request, response, and local-file roles.
misconceptions: The internet and the web are interchangeable names for the same system.
masteryEvidence: A request trace identifies the browser, network, server, response, and rendered document without confusing them with local storage access.

competencies.statement: Trace how a browser turns saved HTML source into a document tree, accessibility information, layout, and pixels.
misconceptions: The browser displays source text directly without parsing, constructing objects, or recovering from errors.
masteryEvidence: A learner predicts and verifies how a source change affects the DOM and rendered page.

competencies.statement: Use browser developer tools to inspect source-derived structure, styles, layout, accessibility, and network evidence.
misconceptions: Changing a value in developer tools permanently edits the saved project source.
masteryEvidence: A defect report links an observed problem to specific DevTools evidence, a saved-source cause, and a verified repair.

competencies.statement: Find, compare, and verify technical information using primary specifications and official documentation.
misconceptions: The first search result is current and authoritative because it ranks above primary documentation.
masteryEvidence: A technical decision cites a current primary source and connects the documented claim to reproducible browser evidence.

competencies.statement: Create a valid HTML document with doctype, language, metadata, title, and viewport configuration.
misconceptions: A fragment containing a div is equivalent to a complete deployable document.
masteryEvidence: An independently authored page passes document-structure checks.

competencies.statement: Choose elements by meaning and nest, open, and close them to form a valid document tree.
misconceptions: Indentation alone creates parent and child relationships in HTML.
masteryEvidence: A DOM inspection matches the intended content hierarchy.

competencies.statement: Use global, boolean, enumerated, and element-specific attributes with valid syntax and purpose.
misconceptions: Every attribute requires an arbitrary text value to become active.
masteryEvidence: Attributes in an independent build match specification-defined behavior.

competencies.statement: Structure prose, headings, quotations, code, and lists so relationships remain explicit.
misconceptions: Heading rank should be selected to achieve a desired font size.
masteryEvidence: A content outline communicates sections without relying on CSS.

competencies.statement: Create understandable links using correct absolute, relative, fragment, email, and telephone URLs.
misconceptions: Link text such as click here remains clear outside its surrounding paragraph.
masteryEvidence: All project links resolve and expose destination purpose.

competencies.statement: Embed responsive images with dimensions and alternative text appropriate to image purpose.
misconceptions: Every image needs a detailed description even when it is purely decorative.
masteryEvidence: An image audit classifies informative, functional, complex, and decorative cases correctly.

competencies.statement: Use inline and external SVG appropriately while preserving accessible names and scalable rendering.
misconceptions: All SVG graphics automatically receive an accessible name from their path data.
masteryEvidence: A meaningful SVG is named and a decorative SVG is hidden correctly.

competencies.statement: Provide usable audio with native controls, suitable sources, fallback, and transcript support.
misconceptions: An audio element is accessible as soon as a source file loads.
masteryEvidence: A media build includes controls, fallback, and equivalent text access.

competencies.statement: Provide usable video with dimensions, controls, multiple sources, captions, and fallback content.
misconceptions: Burned-in visual text replaces synchronized captions for all users.
masteryEvidence: A video player exposes captions and remains usable without autoplay.

competencies.statement: Embed external documents with a descriptive title, constrained permissions, and responsive sizing.
misconceptions: An iframe title changes the visible title displayed inside the embedded page.
masteryEvidence: An embed audit verifies naming, permissions, loading behavior, and layout.

competencies.statement: Use validators, browser tools, and source inspection to locate and correct HTML conformance errors.
misconceptions: If a browser renders a page, its source must be valid and interoperable.
masteryEvidence: A defect report identifies root cause rather than only visible symptoms.

competencies.statement: Use header, nav, main, aside, section, article, and footer according to content relationships.
misconceptions: Semantic containers are interchangeable styled replacements for div elements.
masteryEvidence: A landmark audit finds one main region and purposeful navigation and complementary regions.

competencies.statement: Create a logical heading hierarchy that names pages and sections without skipped structural reasoning.
misconceptions: Each section must restart at h1 regardless of its place in the page hierarchy.
masteryEvidence: A heading outline remains coherent when styles are removed.

competencies.statement: Use emphasis, importance, definitions, abbreviations, citations, and code semantics accurately.
misconceptions: Strong and em exist only to apply bold and italic presentation.
masteryEvidence: Text-level elements communicate the intended linguistic meaning.

competencies.statement: Represent dates, contact information, quotations, and citations with suitable semantic elements.
misconceptions: The address element can wrap any postal address anywhere on a page.
masteryEvidence: Machines and people can identify dates, contacts, and quoted sources.

competencies.statement: Use details and summary for native disclosures when their behavior matches the user need.
misconceptions: Any custom dropdown should be replaced with details regardless of interaction requirements.
masteryEvidence: A disclosure choice is justified by semantics, keyboard behavior, and task needs.

competencies.statement: Evaluate whether element nesting follows HTML content models and preserves valid meaning.
misconceptions: If elements look correct, invalid parent-child relationships have no consequences.
masteryEvidence: A validator and DOM inspection confirm valid content relationships.

competencies.statement: Prefer native HTML behavior before adding roles, scripts, or custom interaction patterns.
misconceptions: Custom elements are more professional than native controls because they allow more styling.
masteryEvidence: A design review selects the simplest native element that satisfies the interaction.

competencies.statement: Choose form structure, action, method, and control names that represent a coherent data submission.
misconceptions: A visible input value is submitted even when its control has no name.
masteryEvidence: Submitted form data contains the intended names and values.

competencies.statement: Give every form control a persistent accessible label and connect shared instructions and errors.
misconceptions: Placeholder text provides an equivalent replacement for a visible label.
masteryEvidence: Keyboard and screen-reader checks expose names and instructions for every control.

competencies.statement: Choose input types and input modes that provide appropriate semantics, validation, and device keyboards.
misconceptions: All short text values should use type text because other types only change appearance.
masteryEvidence: Controls use purpose-specific types without preventing valid international input.

competencies.statement: Group related controls with fieldset and legend and use select, radio, and checkbox semantics correctly.
misconceptions: Visually adjacent radio buttons automatically form an announced group.
masteryEvidence: An options task exposes group purpose and independent versus exclusive choices.

competencies.statement: Apply required, length, range, pattern, and autocomplete attributes without hostile validation rules.
misconceptions: A restrictive regular expression is always the strongest way to improve data quality.
masteryEvidence: Valid realistic data passes while incomplete or impossible data receives specific guidance.

competencies.statement: Represent genuinely two-dimensional data with caption, row groups, rows, headers, and data cells.
misconceptions: Tables are an efficient general-purpose tool for aligning page layout.
masteryEvidence: A data relationship remains understandable with styles disabled.

competencies.statement: Use scope and explicit header associations when necessary so cells retain row and column context.
misconceptions: Bold first-row cells provide the same programmatic relationship as header cells.
masteryEvidence: A table inspection identifies the headers announced for representative cells.

competencies.statement: Test forms and tables with keyboard, zoom, validation states, narrow widths, and assistive semantics.
misconceptions: Successful mouse submission proves the form is accessible and robust.
masteryEvidence: A test record includes multiple input methods and failure states.

competencies.statement: Explain how environmental barriers interact with visual, auditory, motor, speech, cognitive, and neurological needs.
misconceptions: Accessibility is a niche feature for a small fixed category of users.
masteryEvidence: A barrier analysis connects design decisions to multiple access needs.

competencies.statement: Relate DOM semantics, accessible names, roles, states, and properties to the accessibility tree.
misconceptions: Screen readers announce exactly the visible text and ignore programmatic semantics.
masteryEvidence: A DevTools accessibility inspection predicts the announced control information.

competencies.statement: Preserve logical keyboard order, visible focus, native activation, and safe skip navigation.
misconceptions: Adding positive tabindex values repairs any keyboard sequence.
masteryEvidence: All interactive tasks complete by keyboard with a visible predictable focus path.

competencies.statement: Create concise accessible names and supporting descriptions without duplicate or conflicting labels.
misconceptions: Adding aria-label improves every element even when visible text already names it.
masteryEvidence: Name computation for representative controls matches user-facing purpose.

competencies.statement: Apply ARIA roles, states, and properties only when native semantics cannot express required behavior.
misconceptions: ARIA attributes automatically implement keyboard interaction and state changes.
masteryEvidence: A custom pattern includes accurate semantics, state updates, and keyboard behavior.

competencies.statement: Expose validation errors and dynamic status changes in text, focus flow, and appropriate live regions.
misconceptions: Color and a red border give every learner enough error information.
masteryEvidence: Error recovery works with screen reader, keyboard, zoom, and repeated submission.

competencies.statement: Provide captions, transcripts, audio description planning, and non-media alternatives based on content.
misconceptions: Machine-generated captions need no review before publication.
masteryEvidence: Media alternatives convey equivalent information with verified timing and accuracy.

competencies.statement: Combine automated checks with keyboard, zoom, forced-color, reduced-motion, and assistive-technology review.
misconceptions: A perfect automated score proves conformance and usable access.
masteryEvidence: An accessibility report distinguishes automated evidence from manual findings.

competencies.statement: Write valid rules, selectors, declarations, properties, values, comments, and at-rules.
misconceptions: Browsers stop processing an entire stylesheet after one invalid declaration.
masteryEvidence: A stylesheet parses and invalid experiments are diagnosed with browser evidence.

competencies.statement: Choose external, embedded, and inline CSS based on reuse, performance, and maintenance constraints.
misconceptions: Inline styles are best for reusable design because they have high specificity.
masteryEvidence: A project uses an external stylesheet and justifies any exception.

competencies.statement: Select elements with appropriately scoped type, class, ID, compound, and combinator selectors.
misconceptions: More specific selectors are always safer and easier to maintain.
masteryEvidence: Component selectors remain predictable without unnecessary specificity.

competencies.statement: Reason about cascade origin, importance, layers, specificity, scope, and source order.
misconceptions: Specificity alone determines every winning declaration.
masteryEvidence: A cascade trace correctly identifies why one declaration wins.

competencies.statement: Use inheritance, initial values, unset, revert, and currentColor deliberately.
misconceptions: Every CSS property inherits from the parent by default.
masteryEvidence: A component inherits text properties while isolating non-inherited layout.

competencies.statement: Predict and control content, padding, border, margin, box sizing, and margin collapse.
misconceptions: Declared width always equals the final border-box width.
masteryEvidence: Measured layout dimensions match a written box-model calculation.

competencies.statement: Choose block, inline, inline-block, flow-root, and hidden display behavior based on layout needs.
misconceptions: Display none hides content visually while preserving it for assistive technology.
masteryEvidence: Content participates in the intended formatting context and access tree.

competencies.statement: Style list markers and spacing without removing list meaning or scanability.
misconceptions: Removing bullets always removes list semantics from every browser and screen reader.
masteryEvidence: A styled task list preserves structure and readable marker alignment.

competencies.statement: Style links with distinguishable default, visited, hover, focus, and active states.
misconceptions: Hover is sufficient feedback because every device can emulate it.
masteryEvidence: Links remain identifiable without color alone and all interaction states are visible.

competencies.statement: Compose background colors, images, sizing, positions, gradients, borders, radii, and shadows responsibly.
misconceptions: A background image communicates essential content as reliably as an img element.
masteryEvidence: Decorative layers remain legible and do not carry required information.

competencies.statement: Translate stakeholder requests and user research into prioritized user goals and testable tasks.
misconceptions: The stakeholder feature list is equivalent to evidence about user needs.
masteryEvidence: A design brief identifies users, jobs, constraints, and success evidence.

competencies.statement: Create visual and structural hierarchy through content order, headings, scale, contrast, and grouping.
misconceptions: Making every important item larger creates a clearer hierarchy.
masteryEvidence: Representative users can locate primary and secondary actions quickly.

competencies.statement: Use consistent spacing, alignment, proximity, and whitespace to communicate relationships.
misconceptions: Whitespace is unused room that should be filled to maximize value.
masteryEvidence: A spacing audit maps repeated distances to a deliberate scale.

competencies.statement: Choose color and typography systems that support brand, readability, states, and accessibility.
misconceptions: A brand color must be used for text even when it fails contrast.
masteryEvidence: Design tokens produce readable text and distinguishable states.

competencies.statement: Make controls, status, errors, and navigation consistent while providing immediate useful feedback.
misconceptions: Consistency means every component must look identical regardless of purpose.
masteryEvidence: A user can predict behavior and recover from errors across the interface.

competencies.statement: Create low-to-high fidelity prototypes and test assumptions before expensive implementation.
misconceptions: A polished mockup is proof that users can complete the intended task.
masteryEvidence: A test report links observed behavior to a revised design decision.

competencies.statement: Use absolute CSS units only where their physical or pixel-relative behavior matches the medium.
misconceptions: CSS pixels always correspond to one physical device pixel.
masteryEvidence: Unit choices are justified by output medium and zoom behavior.

competencies.statement: Use rem, em, ch, lh, and related units with accurate reference contexts.
misconceptions: An em value always refers to the root font size.
masteryEvidence: Typography and component spacing scale predictably under user font changes.

competencies.statement: Predict percentage sizing by identifying the correct containing-block reference.
misconceptions: Every percentage resolves against viewport width.
masteryEvidence: A sizing explanation names the property-specific percentage basis.

competencies.statement: Use viewport and dynamic viewport units without obscuring content or fighting mobile browser UI.
misconceptions: One hundred vh always equals the visible mobile screen height.
masteryEvidence: A full-height layout survives mobile browser controls and zoom.

competencies.statement: Compose min, max, clamp, and calc expressions to create bounded fluid sizes.
misconceptions: Fluid values need no minimum or maximum because every viewport scales proportionally.
masteryEvidence: Text and spacing grow smoothly while staying within readable bounds.

competencies.statement: Use link, pointer, keyboard, target, and focus pseudo-classes without input-mode assumptions.
misconceptions: Hover and focus represent the same interaction for every user.
masteryEvidence: Controls show distinct useful states for mouse, touch, and keyboard.

competencies.statement: Use child, type, empty, root, and functional structural selectors against the actual DOM.
misconceptions: Nth-child counts only elements that match the selector before it.
masteryEvidence: A selector trace correctly predicts which nodes match.

competencies.statement: Style required, optional, valid, invalid, checked, disabled, and indeterminate form states responsibly.
misconceptions: Invalid styling should appear before a learner has interacted with a blank form.
masteryEvidence: Validation styling is timed and explained without color-only cues.

competencies.statement: Use is, where, not, and has while controlling specificity and browser support.
misconceptions: The where function adds the specificity of its most specific argument.
masteryEvidence: A selector is simplified without creating accidental specificity.

competencies.statement: Use before, after, marker, selection, placeholder, and first-letter for presentation, not essential content.
misconceptions: Generated content is a reliable place for required instructions.
masteryEvidence: The page remains understandable when generated presentation is unavailable.

competencies.statement: Represent color with named, hexadecimal, RGB, HSL, and modern color syntax while understanding alpha.
misconceptions: Different color notations create inherently different displayed colors.
masteryEvidence: Equivalent color values are recognized and alpha composition is predicted.

competencies.statement: Use hue, saturation, lightness, temperature, and harmony as tools rather than fixed aesthetic rules.
misconceptions: A mathematically harmonious palette automatically communicates usable hierarchy.
masteryEvidence: Palette choices connect to content purpose and user testing.

competencies.statement: Verify text, component, focus, and graphical contrast against applicable WCAG criteria.
misconceptions: A contrast checker for one hex pair proves contrast in every state and gradient location.
masteryEvidence: All interactive states and text sizes have recorded contrast evidence.

competencies.statement: Communicate meaning through text, shape, position, or iconography in addition to color.
misconceptions: Adding a second shade is enough to avoid a color-only distinction.
masteryEvidence: Status remains understandable in grayscale and forced colors.

competencies.statement: Build linear, radial, conic, and repeating gradients with controlled stops and readable overlays.
misconceptions: A gradient is an image element and needs an alt attribute.
masteryEvidence: Decorative gradients preserve contrast and do not encode required data alone.

competencies.statement: Normalize form control typography and color without erasing useful native behavior.
misconceptions: Form controls inherit every font and color property automatically.
masteryEvidence: Controls match the design system while remaining recognizable.

competencies.statement: Lay out labels, controls, hints, and actions with readable measure and generous target sizes.
misconceptions: A compact desktop form can be proportionally shrunk for touch screens.
masteryEvidence: The form reflows at narrow widths with usable targets and no horizontal scroll.

competencies.statement: Create strong focus and focus-within treatments that survive contrast modes and are not clipped.
misconceptions: Removing outlines is safe when hover styling exists.
masteryEvidence: Every control has a visible keyboard focus indicator.

competencies.statement: Style errors, success, help, required state, and disabled state with multiple cues.
misconceptions: Red borders alone provide clear error recovery.
masteryEvidence: Each state has text and programmatic meaning as well as visual treatment.

competencies.statement: Decide when to retain native control appearance and when a fully implemented custom pattern is justified.
misconceptions: Custom checkboxes need only a hidden native input and decorative box.
masteryEvidence: A control review covers semantics, keyboard, state, zoom, and forced colors.

competencies.statement: Handle autofill, placeholder, reduced motion, and browser-specific form states without hiding data.
misconceptions: Autofill colors can be overridden without checking readability or user expectations.
masteryEvidence: Completed fields remain readable and state changes respect preferences.

competencies.statement: Choose visible, hidden, clip, auto, and scroll overflow while preserving content and focus access.
misconceptions: Overflow hidden is a harmless way to repair any layout that extends beyond a card.
masteryEvidence: Long content, zoom, and keyboard focus remain reachable.

competencies.statement: Use min and max sizes, aspect ratio, and intrinsic keywords to constrain components safely.
misconceptions: Fixed height is the most reliable way to keep cards aligned.
masteryEvidence: Components accept longer text without overlap or clipping.

competencies.statement: Compose translate, rotate, scale, skew, transform origin, and individual transforms predictably.
misconceptions: Transforms move surrounding layout boxes and create space for neighbors.
masteryEvidence: A geometry explanation distinguishes visual transform from document flow.

competencies.statement: Use visual filters with restraint while preserving performance, contrast, and content meaning.
misconceptions: A brightness filter reliably repairs contrast for arbitrary images and text.
masteryEvidence: Filtered content remains legible and has a non-filter fallback.

competencies.statement: Use box and text shadows to support hierarchy without replacing boundaries or focus indicators.
misconceptions: More shadows always make interactive elevation clearer.
masteryEvidence: Depth cues remain understandable in high contrast and without shadow.

competencies.statement: Recognize how transforms, filters, opacity, and containment create stacking and containing contexts.
misconceptions: Z-index values compare globally across every element on the page.
masteryEvidence: A stacking bug is explained from context boundaries and paint order.

competencies.statement: Debug clipping, stacking, transform, and filter defects through reduced cases and computed evidence.
misconceptions: Raising z-index repeatedly is a dependable stacking-context fix.
masteryEvidence: A reduced test case identifies the property that creates the defect.

competencies.statement: Create a flex formatting context and identify main axis, cross axis, and direction.
misconceptions: The main axis is always horizontal and the cross axis always vertical.
masteryEvidence: A layout explanation follows the configured writing mode and direction.

competencies.statement: Predict flex base size, free space, grow, shrink, minimum size, and final item dimensions.
misconceptions: Flex one means each item always receives exactly the same width.
masteryEvidence: A sizing calculation explains why items grow or shrink differently.

competencies.statement: Use justify, align, self-alignment, and gaps based on the correct axis and distribution need.
misconceptions: Justify-content vertically centers items in every flex container.
masteryEvidence: Alignment rules match axis and leave content reachable.

competencies.statement: Use wrapping and flexible bases to create resilient rows without device-specific item counts.
misconceptions: Flex-wrap alone guarantees every card has a usable minimum width.
masteryEvidence: Cards wrap before content becomes cramped or overflows.

competencies.statement: Avoid visual reordering that separates reading, focus, and visual sequences.
misconceptions: Changing CSS order also changes screen-reader and keyboard sequence.
masteryEvidence: Source order remains logical at every layout.

competencies.statement: Choose Flexbox, Grid, block flow, or another method based on dimensional and content constraints.
misconceptions: Flexbox is the modern replacement for every older layout mechanism.
masteryEvidence: A layout decision names dimensions, alignment, wrapping, and source-order needs.

competencies.statement: Build resilient font stacks and understand generic families, fallback metrics, and system fonts.
misconceptions: A downloaded web font displays before it loads with no layout consequences.
masteryEvidence: Text remains readable and stable when preferred fonts fail.

competencies.statement: Declare web fonts with suitable formats, weights, styles, ranges, and display behavior.
misconceptions: One regular font file can synthesize every weight and style with equal quality.
masteryEvidence: Requested styles map to real resources and loading behavior is tested.

competencies.statement: Create a bounded type scale that communicates hierarchy without breaking at zoom or narrow widths.
misconceptions: Fixed pixel font sizes provide the most consistent accessible typography.
masteryEvidence: Heading and body sizes remain proportional and user-scalable.

competencies.statement: Control line height, measure, paragraph spacing, letter spacing, and word spacing for readability.
misconceptions: Tightly packed long lines make reading faster because more text fits on screen.
masteryEvidence: Prose stays within readable measure across viewports.

competencies.statement: Handle long words, URLs, wrapping, hyphenation, truncation, and vertical writing without data loss.
misconceptions: Ellipsis is safe for any content because users understand hidden text exists.
masteryEvidence: Unexpected content remains available and does not force page overflow.

competencies.statement: Use relative units and clamp to adapt typography while honoring user settings and content needs.
misconceptions: Viewport units alone are ideal because they always scale with device size.
masteryEvidence: Type scales fluidly within tested readable bounds.

competencies.statement: Support text resize and 400 percent zoom without lost content, controls, or two-dimensional scrolling.
misconceptions: A desktop layout can remain fixed because browser zoom simply scales the screenshot.
masteryEvidence: Critical workflows complete at high zoom and narrow effective widths.

competencies.statement: Choose display, visibility, opacity, clipping, and visually hidden patterns with accurate access effects.
misconceptions: Opacity zero removes an element from keyboard and accessibility navigation.
masteryEvidence: Hidden-state behavior matches visual, layout, focus, and accessibility intent.

competencies.statement: Protect visible focus indicators from resets, clipping, overlays, and insufficient contrast.
misconceptions: A subtle color shift is enough focus evidence for every control.
masteryEvidence: Focus appearance remains visible on all component backgrounds.

competencies.statement: Respond to reduced motion, contrast, color scheme, transparency, and forced-color preferences appropriately.
misconceptions: Preference media queries should disable all useful feedback and state changes.
masteryEvidence: Adaptations preserve meaning while reducing unwanted effects.

competencies.statement: Provide adequate target size, spacing, orientation flexibility, and alternatives to path-based gestures.
misconceptions: Small icons are usable when their SVG graphic is visually sharp.
masteryEvidence: Touch testing confirms target area and accidental activation risk.

competencies.statement: Add automated and manual accessibility regression checks to component and project completion.
misconceptions: Accessibility needs to be checked only once after all visual work is finished.
masteryEvidence: Every cumulative build records repeated access checks.

competencies.statement: Use normal block and inline flow as the resilient baseline before removing content from flow.
misconceptions: Professional layouts require positioning every major region explicitly.
masteryEvidence: A layout remains readable before enhancement.

competencies.statement: Use floats for inline content wrapping and contain or clear them when appropriate.
misconceptions: Floats are a general two-dimensional page-layout system equivalent to Grid.
masteryEvidence: Text wraps around media without collapsing its container.

competencies.statement: Use relative positioning for local offsets and containing-block creation without confusing flow space.
misconceptions: Relative offsets cause following siblings to move into the vacated space.
masteryEvidence: An offset element retains its original flow allocation.

competencies.statement: Position an element against the intended containing block while reserving necessary content space.
misconceptions: Absolutely positioned content always uses the viewport as its reference.
masteryEvidence: A badge remains anchored to its component across layout changes.

competencies.statement: Use fixed and sticky positioning with offsets, scroll containers, safe areas, and obstruction testing.
misconceptions: Sticky positioning works independently of ancestor overflow and scroll containers.
masteryEvidence: Navigation stays useful without covering targets or zoomed content.

competencies.statement: Predict stacking contexts and paint order created by positioning, opacity, transforms, isolation, and containment.
misconceptions: The numerically largest z-index must appear above every page element.
masteryEvidence: A layer map explains actual paint order across nested contexts.

competencies.statement: Use presence and exact-value attribute selectors for semantic types and states.
misconceptions: An attribute selector changes the underlying semantic state it matches.
masteryEvidence: Styles reflect truthful document attributes.

competencies.statement: Use token, hyphenated language, and case-sensitivity matching with accurate value models.
misconceptions: The contains operator is interchangeable with token matching.
masteryEvidence: Selectors distinguish tokens, prefixes, and language subcodes correctly.

competencies.statement: Use prefix, suffix, and substring matching only when stable attribute conventions justify them.
misconceptions: Substring selectors remain safe when filenames and URL formats change arbitrarily.
masteryEvidence: A selector documents the stable convention it relies on.

competencies.statement: Style data and ARIA state without using CSS as the source of truth for interaction behavior.
misconceptions: Changing generated content or color updates an ARIA state automatically.
masteryEvidence: State styles match DOM state and scripted behavior.

competencies.statement: Start with a robust narrow layout and add enhancements only when content and space support them.
misconceptions: Mobile first means designing only for a standard phone width before desktop.
masteryEvidence: Base styles work at the smallest supported width without override dependencies.

competencies.statement: Choose breakpoints where content or interaction needs change instead of matching device brands.
misconceptions: A complete design needs separate breakpoints for every popular device model.
masteryEvidence: Breakpoint notes identify concrete content failure evidence.

competencies.statement: Compose range, preference, pointer, hover, orientation, and resolution queries with sensible fallbacks.
misconceptions: Viewport width is the only environmental condition relevant to responsive design.
masteryEvidence: The interface adapts to size and user capability without removing access.

competencies.statement: Use intrinsic sizing, picture, srcset, sizes, formats, and art direction to serve suitable images.
misconceptions: CSS width alone prevents a browser from downloading an unnecessarily large source.
masteryEvidence: Image candidates and rendered dimensions fit tested viewports.

competencies.statement: Make video, iframe, SVG, and other embedded media preserve ratio and avoid page overflow.
misconceptions: Setting width one hundred percent always preserves an embed aspect ratio.
masteryEvidence: All embeds resize without distortion or horizontal scrolling.

competencies.statement: Use containment and container queries for components whose layout depends on local available space.
misconceptions: Container queries are a direct replacement for every viewport media query.
masteryEvidence: A reusable component adapts correctly in different parent layouts.

competencies.statement: Verify responsive behavior across content lengths, viewports, zoom, input modes, orientations, and preferences.
misconceptions: Testing three screenshots proves a responsive system is complete.
masteryEvidence: A test matrix records behavior and defects across meaningful conditions.

competencies.statement: Define and consume custom properties with valid names, values, and var references.
misconceptions: Custom properties are replaced before cascade and never inherit.
masteryEvidence: Tokens resolve predictably in component declarations.

competencies.statement: Reason about custom-property inheritance, cascade, scope, and local component overrides.
misconceptions: A root token cannot be overridden safely inside a component.
masteryEvidence: A local theme changes one subtree without unintended global effects.

competencies.statement: Use nested fallbacks while understanding missing values and invalid-at-computed-value behavior.
misconceptions: A var fallback repairs every syntactically invalid computed value.
masteryEvidence: A failure experiment distinguishes missing and invalid values.

competencies.statement: Organize primitive, semantic, and component tokens without hiding meaning behind arbitrary names.
misconceptions: Every raw CSS value should become a custom property regardless of reuse.
masteryEvidence: Token names communicate decisions and support coherent themes.

competencies.statement: Register selected custom properties with syntax, inheritance, and initial values when typed behavior helps.
misconceptions: Every custom property needs an at-property registration to work.
masteryEvidence: A registered value animates or validates for a justified use case.

competencies.statement: Create a grid formatting context and distinguish explicit and implicit tracks.
misconceptions: Only explicitly declared cells can participate in Grid layout.
masteryEvidence: A grid inspection identifies explicit and generated tracks.

competencies.statement: Size tracks with fixed, flexible, intrinsic, minmax, fit-content, and repeat functions.
misconceptions: One fractional unit means one fixed fraction of viewport width.
masteryEvidence: Track sizing adapts without forcing content below intrinsic minimums.

competencies.statement: Place items with lines, spans, negative indices, and auto-placement while preserving logical source order.
misconceptions: Explicit visual placement changes reading and focus order.
masteryEvidence: A complex layout retains meaningful DOM order.

competencies.statement: Use named grid areas for understandable layouts and redefine them safely at breakpoints.
misconceptions: Area names can form non-rectangular shapes across a template.
masteryEvidence: Templates are valid rectangles and reflect content priority.

competencies.statement: Choose auto-fit or auto-fill with minmax for content-driven repeating tracks.
misconceptions: Auto-fit and auto-fill always produce identical empty-track behavior.
masteryEvidence: A gallery decision accounts for empty and collapsed tracks.

competencies.statement: Align the grid, tracks, and items using the correct content and self-alignment properties.
misconceptions: Align-items controls the placement of the entire grid in its container.
masteryEvidence: Alignment changes are predicted at both grid and item levels.

competencies.statement: Use subgrid where supported to align nested component content to shared parent tracks.
misconceptions: Nested grids automatically inherit parent track lines.
masteryEvidence: A card collection aligns internal regions without duplicated track values.

competencies.statement: Debug cascade, selector, layout, overflow, and rendering failures with a repeatable evidence-driven method.
misconceptions: Trying random property changes is faster than isolating the smallest failing case.
masteryEvidence: A defect report records reproduction, hypothesis, evidence, fix, and regression check.

competencies.statement: Use transitions for meaningful changes between known states with suitable properties and timing.
misconceptions: Transition all is harmless because browsers choose only cheap properties.
masteryEvidence: A state transition animates only properties that improve understanding.

competencies.statement: Define, name, and apply keyframe animations with clear start, intermediate, and end states.
misconceptions: Keyframe percentages represent elapsed milliseconds rather than relative progress.
masteryEvidence: An animation reaches intended states and keeps stable fill behavior.

competencies.statement: Choose duration, delay, iteration, direction, fill, and easing based on purpose and user control.
misconceptions: Longer or bouncier animation always makes an interface feel more engaging.
masteryEvidence: Motion timing supports feedback without delaying the task.

competencies.statement: Prefer transform and opacity where suitable and measure main-thread, layout, paint, and compositing effects.
misconceptions: Transform and opacity are always free and need no performance verification.
masteryEvidence: A performance trace supports the animation implementation decision.

competencies.statement: Provide reduced-motion alternatives that preserve status and meaning instead of merely shortening duration.
misconceptions: A very fast animation automatically satisfies reduced-motion needs.
masteryEvidence: The preferred reduction removes nonessential motion while retaining feedback.

competencies.statement: Diagnose animation precedence, interpolation, transform composition, and missing-state defects.
misconceptions: An animation always combines automatically with a transform declared elsewhere.
masteryEvidence: A reduced case identifies the exact conflicting animation or transform rule.

modules.title: Computers, Files, Browsers, and Evidence
modules.description: Trace a page from input and storage through local and network loading, browser construction, developer-tool evidence, and verified technical documentation.
objectives: Trace a page from user input and stored data through operating-system, browser, graphics, and display responsibilities.
Organize a portable project and diagnose extensions, case mismatches, moved files, and broken relative paths.
Distinguish local-file loading from browser-server request and response behavior.
Use browser evidence and primary sources to test claims about source, DOM, accessibility information, layout, and pixels.

modules.title: Documents, Content, Links, and Media
modules.description: Build and audit public-information documents from the shell outward: valid trees, purposeful attributes, explicit text structure, trustworthy destinations, equivalent images and media, constrained embeds, and validator-led debugging.
objectives: Author a complete portable HTML document and explain its source-to-DOM relationships.
Structure headings, prose, lists, links, images, vectors, audio, video, and embeds by purpose rather than appearance.
Carry a real artifact through cumulative code increments while transferring the same skills to unfamiliar labs.
Use source validation, browser inspection, accessibility evidence, and retesting to diagnose root causes.

modules.title: Semantic HTML and Content Relationships
modules.description: Turn valid tags into a durable meaning system: landmarks, outlines, text semantics, dates and sources, native disclosure, content models, and native-first interaction decisions.
objectives: Choose landmark and heading structures from content relationships and verify the resulting navigation map.
Encode text, dates, contacts, quotations, and sources so their meaning survives presentation changes.
Evaluate content models and browser recovery with validation plus source-to-DOM evidence.
Prefer complete native behavior contracts before adding roles, scripts, or custom controls.

modules.title: Forms, Validation, and Data Tables
modules.description: Design humane data-entry contracts and resilient two-dimensional reports, then verify names, groups, constraints, keyboard behavior, reflow, and announced relationships.
objectives: Design form action, method, names, labels, instructions, types, and groups from a real submission workflow.
Apply native validation that supports realistic input without treating the browser as a security boundary.
Model two-dimensional data with captions, row groups, headers, cells, and durable header associations.
Collect release evidence across keyboard, zoom, narrow widths, invalid states, and assistive semantics.

modules.title: Certification Project: Community Needs Survey
modules.description: Plan, build, audit, and defend a complete community heat-relief survey from a real-world brief using retained HTML, semantic, form, validation, and test skills.
objectives: Create a complete unfamiliar survey without step solutions or disposable practice files.
Keep every earlier contract passing while requirements accumulate across the project.
Defend the release with implementation, behavior, failure-state, and accessibility evidence.

modules.title: HTML Accessibility as an Engineering Practice
modules.description: Remove task barriers through semantic source, predictable keyboard and focus behavior, deliberate name computation, minimal ARIA, recoverable errors, equivalent media, and multi-method test evidence.
objectives: Connect product and environmental barriers to varied permanent, temporary, situational, and overlapping access needs.
Trace DOM source into accessibility-tree names, roles, states, descriptions, and exposed relationships.
Build predictable keyboard routes, accurate control names, minimal ARIA, and recoverable error and status flows.
Provide reviewed media equivalents and combine automated results with task-based manual and assistive checks.

modules.title: Cumulative HTML Review
modules.description: Retrieve the complete HTML foundation in changed production incidents before adding CSS, with cumulative repairs and explicit implementation, failure-state, and accessibility evidence.
objectives: Integrate retained HTML and accessibility skills without introducing new concepts.
Repair production-like regressions while preserving all earlier passing requirements.
Reach CSS study with verified control over document, content, form, table, and accessibility contracts.

modules.title: CSS Syntax, Cascade, and Visual Foundations
modules.description: Move from parsed rules to maintainable external styles, stable selector scope, explicit cascade layers, deliberate inheritance, calculable boxes, meaningful formatting contexts, structured lists, complete link states, and responsible decorative layers.
objectives: Write valid CSS and diagnose parse failures with source, rule, and computed-style evidence.
Choose delivery and selector architecture for reuse without specificity escalation.
Trace cascade, inheritance, box dimensions, and display behavior instead of styling by trial and error.
Preserve list meaning, complete link feedback, contrast, and content equivalence while adding visual design.

modules.title: User-Centered Interface Design
modules.description: Translate evidence into user goals, create structural and visual hierarchy, use spacing and alignment to explain relationships, choose readable color and typography, standardize feedback by purpose, and test assumptions at the cheapest useful prototype fidelity.
objectives: Separate stakeholder requests, research evidence, assumptions, constraints, prioritized user tasks, and observable success.
Create hierarchy and relationship cues whose visual, reading, and focus order agree.
Build readable color, typography, state, and feedback systems that survive real content and access conditions.
Choose prototype fidelity from risk and connect observed behavior to revisions and remaining uncertainty.

modules.title: Sizing, Units, and Intrinsic Constraints
modules.description: Choose CSS units by their real reference context, calculate percentage bases, survive mobile browser viewport changes, and compose bounded fluid text, spacing, and component sizes.
objectives: Justify absolute-unit choices from screen, print, device scale, and zoom behavior.
Calculate rem, em, ch, lh, and percentage values from the correct reference context.
Use small and dynamic viewport units without clipping content behind mobile browser controls.
Compose min, max, clamp, and calc expressions with explicit usable lower and upper bounds.

modules.title: State, Structure, and Generated Presentation
modules.description: Style input and navigation states without modality assumptions, trace structural matching against the DOM, time form feedback responsibly, control functional-selector specificity, and keep pseudo-element content nonessential.
objectives: Differentiate link, pointer, keyboard, activation, group-focus, and fragment-target states across input modes.
Predict structural pseudo-class matching from the actual sibling and element-type sequence.
Style form validity, selection, and availability with humane timing and non-color feedback.
Use is, where, not, has, and pseudo-elements without accidental specificity or essential generated content.

modules.title: Color Systems, Contrast, and Gradients
modules.description: Represent and composite color accurately, build palettes from functional roles, verify rendered contrast across states, preserve meaning without author colors, and compose decorative gradients with solid fallbacks.
objectives: Recognize equivalent color formats and predict alpha compositing against actual layered backgrounds.
Use hue, chroma, lightness, temperature, and harmony as purpose-driven exploration tools rather than usability guarantees.
Record text, component, graphic, and focus contrast evidence across every meaningful state and painted location.
Preserve status and selection meaning without color and keep gradients decorative with readable fallbacks.

modules.title: Resilient Form Interface Design
modules.description: Normalize controls without erasing native behavior, build readable reflowing layouts, preserve strong focus, communicate every state with multiple cues, choose native before custom, and respect browser autofill and motion preferences.
objectives: Normalize typography, color, and sizing across form controls while preserving useful platform behavior.
Lay out labels, controls, help, errors, and actions for touch, keyboard, zoom, narrow width, and translation.
Build focus and state treatments that remain visible, recoverable, and understandable without color alone.
Retain native controls unless a custom pattern has complete semantic, keyboard, state, form, zoom, and forced-color evidence.

modules.title: Overflow, Effects, and Transform Geometry
modules.description: Preserve access through overflow, constrain variable content intrinsically, distinguish transformed paint from layout, use filters and shadows as secondary effects, map stacking contexts, and debug combined failures through reduced evidence.
objectives: Choose intentional overflow behavior while preserving long content, zoom, scrolling, and keyboard focus.
Use intrinsic min, max, aspect-ratio, and content-driven block sizing instead of fixed-height clipping.
Predict transform geometry and keep filters, shadows, and decorative paint nonessential and preference-safe.
Explain stacking-context boundaries and isolate effect defects with reduced cases and computed evidence.

modules.title: One-Dimensional Layout with Flexbox
modules.description: Map axes from writing mode and direction, calculate flexible sizing, align along the correct axis, wrap from content constraints, preserve source order, and choose Flexbox only for suitable one-dimensional relationships.
objectives: Identify flex containers, direct items, main and cross axes under changed direction and writing modes.
Calculate bases, positive or negative free space, grow, scaled shrink, and min or max clamping.
Use alignment, gap, flexible bases, and wrapping from content constraints rather than device labels.
Keep source, reading, focus, and visual order aligned and choose the correct layout method by dimensional need.

modules.title: Certification Project: Volunteer Shift Deck
modules.description: Build and verify a new semantic volunteer shift-card artifact using retained box, Flexbox, state, contrast, preference, source-order, and layout-method decisions.
objectives: Create a complete unfamiliar card collection from a service brief.
Keep all requirements cumulative across alternating design and code checkpoints.
Defend the finished project through multi-state accessibility and layout evidence.

modules.title: Readable and Responsive Typography
modules.description: Build metric-aware fallbacks, map real web-font resources, create bounded type scales, control reading measure and rhythm, preserve unexpected text, and adapt typography without overriding user settings.
objectives: Build resilient font stacks and verify layout under preferred, fallback, late, blocked, and user-overridden fonts.
Declare real web-font styles, weights, ranges, formats, and display behavior without synthetic assumptions.
Create readable hierarchy, line height, measure, spacing, wrapping, and content-driven block growth.
Use relative bounded fluid type that survives zoom, root-size changes, long content, and narrow widths.

modules.title: Accessible CSS under Real User Preferences
modules.description: Preserve complete tasks under zoom and reflow, choose hidden-state behavior accurately, protect focus, adapt to user preferences, create generous targets, and repeat accessibility regression checks after every visual change.
objectives: Complete critical workflows at high zoom and narrow effective widths without lost content or controls.
Match hidden techniques to visual, layout, focus, hit-test, and accessibility-tree intent.
Protect focus and preserve meaningful feedback under reduced motion, contrast, dark scheme, and forced colors.
Provide generous multi-input targets and attach repeated access evidence to every cumulative build.

modules.title: Flow, Positioning, and Stacking
modules.description: Keep normal flow as the resilient baseline, use floats only for prose wrapping, map relative and absolute containing blocks, test fixed and sticky obstruction, and predict paint order through stacking-context trees.
objectives: Build source-ordered normal flow before enhancing a specific relationship with another layout or positioning method.
Use floats for inline media wrapping and contain or clear them without turning them into a page grid.
Distinguish relative flow allocation from absolute containing-block geometry and reserve necessary space.
Map sticky scroll containers, safe areas, obstructions, and nested stacking contexts with evidence.

modules.title: Attribute-Driven Styling
modules.description: Match presence, exact values, tokens, language subcodes, stable URL conventions, data state, and ARIA state accurately while keeping HTML and behavior—not CSS—as the source of truth.
objectives: Use presence and exact-value selectors to reflect truthful semantic type and state attributes.
Distinguish whitespace tokens, hyphenated language prefixes, exact values, and case-sensitivity rules.
Use prefix, suffix, and substring matching only against documented stable conventions.
Style data and ARIA states while keeping interaction logic, semantics, keyboard behavior, and state transitions synchronized.

modules.title: Certification Project: Accessible Resource Inventory
modules.description: Build and verify a new inventory artifact integrating semantic tables, attribute-driven state, localized responsive overflow, non-color meaning, focus, forced colors, and print.
objectives: Create a complete unfamiliar data inventory from a service brief.
Keep table, state, focus, overflow, and output requirements cumulative.
Defend the release with representative header, input, preference, and print evidence.

modules.title: Responsive Systems from Content Out
modules.description: Build complete narrow-first interfaces, choose content breakpoints, query capabilities and preferences safely, deliver suitable images and embeds, adapt components to local space, and verify the result with a multivariable regression matrix.
objectives: Build a complete content-driven base before adding evidence-backed responsive enhancements.
Choose breakpoints and media conditions from content, task, capability, and preference evidence instead of device labels.
Deliver responsive images and embedded media with correct network, ratio, semantic, and fallback behavior.
Use container queries for local component composition and media queries for page and environment decisions.
Verify content length, widths, zoom, orientation, input, preferences, media selection, print, and container placement in a recorded matrix.

modules.title: Certification Project: Developer Field Manual
modules.description: Build an incident-ready, linkable technical manual with semantic information architecture, readable code, responsive and sticky navigation, durable fragment and keyboard behavior, print adaptation, and a reproducible accessibility matrix.
objectives: Organize technical instructions with semantic landmarks, headings, lists, code, tables, warnings, and descriptive links.
Build narrow-first navigation and enhance it with bounded sticky behavior only when content supports it.
Preserve fragment targets, keyboard focus, code access, zoom, forced colors, and print output.
Publish implementation and manual-test evidence suitable for support-engineering handoff.

modules.title: Custom Properties and Design Tokens
modules.description: Trace custom-property resolution through cascade and inheritance, distinguish missing from invalid computed values, organize meaningful token layers, build accessible themes, and register only values that benefit from typed behavior.
objectives: Define and consume custom properties while tracing the winning declaration and computed consumer value.
Use inheritance, scope, and local overrides as intentional component contracts.
Test nested fallbacks separately from present-but-invalid computed values.
Organize primitive, semantic, and component tokens around durable accessible design decisions.
Register selected properties when typed validation, interpolation, or explicit inheritance is justified.

modules.title: Two-Dimensional Grid Layout and CSS Debugging
modules.description: Reason through explicit and implicit grids, intrinsic and flexible track sizing, source-order-safe placement, named areas, auto-fit and auto-fill, alignment levels, subgrid, and a reproducible browser-evidence debugging method.
objectives: Distinguish explicit and implicit Grid structure and choose Grid only for genuine two-dimensional relationships.
Predict intrinsic, fixed, minmax, fit-content, repeat, and flexible track sizing under changed content.
Use lines, spans, auto-placement, and named areas while preserving meaningful DOM, reading, and focus order.
Choose auto-fit or auto-fill and apply content, item, and self alignment on the correct logical axis.
Use subgrid with a usable fallback for shared nested alignment.
Debug cascade, computed values, tracks, overflow, and rendering with a recorded hypothesis and regression cycle.

modules.title: Certification Project: Ethical Product Launch
modules.description: Build an honest nonprofit learning-product launch with bounded claims, accessible evidence and media, explicit consent, resilient forms, content-driven responsive layout, durable states, and a reproducible ethical review matrix.
objectives: Communicate audience value, evidence, limitations, cost, consent, and next actions without manipulative urgency or unsupported outcomes.
Deliver responsive images and demonstration media with captions, transcript, correct candidates, intrinsic dimensions, and ratio behavior.
Build a labeled native interest form with explicit data purpose, optional consent, understandable validation, autofill, and recovery states.
Verify contrast, keyboard, zoom, long content, breakpoints, reduced motion, forced colors, print, and bounded claims.

modules.title: Purposeful and Accessible Motion Systems
modules.description: Use transitions and keyframes for meaningful state feedback, control timelines from purpose, measure real rendering cost, preserve meaning under reduced-motion preferences, and debug animation precedence and transform composition with browser evidence.
objectives: Transition only properties whose interpolation clarifies a real state change without delaying tasks.
Define stable keyframe sequences and choose duration, easing, delay, iteration, direction, fill, and controls deliberately.
Measure layout, paint, compositing, layer, memory, and frame effects instead of assuming animation performance.
Replace nonessential spatial motion with immediate equivalent status cues under reduced-motion preferences.
Debug active animation precedence, shorthand resets, interpolation, fill behavior, keyframe identity, and transform composition.

modules.title: Cumulative CSS Review
modules.description: Retrieve CSS cascade, box, Flexbox, Grid, responsive, accessibility, debugging, and motion skills across sixteen changed production incidents with cumulative repairs and regression evidence.
objectives: Integrate CSS algorithms and accessibility constraints without introducing new concepts.
Diagnose changed production failures from source, computed, layout, interaction, and user evidence.
Repair root causes while preserving all earlier responsive and accessible requirements.

modules.title: Responsive Web Design Certification Exam
modules.description: Demonstrate integrated responsive web design mastery across twenty-two unfamiliar document, semantic, form, accessibility, cascade, box, Flexbox, Grid, responsive media, motion, and debugging incidents.
objectives: Integrate HTML, CSS, accessibility, responsive media, layout algorithms, motion, and debugging without guided examples.
Apply twenty-two cumulative implementation repairs while preserving every earlier exam requirement.
Defend cause-level decisions with user consequences, failure states, and multivariable regression evidence.
